note: Lab 2 3D Anatomy Explorer manifest. GLBs are stored LOCALLY at the paths below (never hotlinked). Until a real GLB is installed, the viewer shows a clearly-marked render-test object, NOT anatomy. Each structure binds to the model either by `mesh` (named mesh in the GLB) or `hotspot` ([x,y,z] in model space). Fill `mesh`/`hotspot` when the real GLB lands — no viewer changes needed.

models.title: Heart
models.source: PENDING (Codex to supply — e.g. Z-Anatomy CC BY-SA 4.0 or a verified NIH 3D model)

structures.name: Right atrium
structures.function: Receives deoxygenated blood from the venae cavae; pumps it to the right ventricle.

structures.name: Right ventricle
structures.function: Pumps deoxygenated blood to the lungs via the pulmonary trunk.

structures.name: Left atrium
structures.function: Receives oxygenated blood from the pulmonary veins; pumps it to the left ventricle.

structures.name: Left ventricle
structures.function: Thickest chamber; pumps oxygenated blood into the aorta to the whole body.

structures.name: Interventricular septum
structures.function: Muscular wall separating the two ventricles.

structures.name: Tricuspid valve
structures.function: Right atrioventricular valve; prevents backflow into the right atrium.

structures.name: Bicuspid (mitral) valve
structures.function: Left atrioventricular valve; prevents backflow into the left atrium.

structures.name: Aorta
structures.function: Great artery carrying oxygenated blood from the left ventricle to the body.

structures.name: Pulmonary trunk
structures.function: Carries deoxygenated blood from the right ventricle to the lungs.

structures.name: Superior vena cava
structures.function: Returns deoxygenated blood from the upper body to the right atrium.

structures.name: Inferior vena cava
structures.function: Returns deoxygenated blood from the lower body to the right atrium.

structures.name: Pulmonary veins
structures.function: Return oxygenated blood from the lungs to the left atrium.

structures.name: Left coronary artery
structures.function: Supplies the left heart wall; branches into anterior interventricular (LAD) and circumflex.

structures.name: Coronary sinus
structures.function: Large vein on the posterior heart draining cardiac veins into the right atrium.

structures.name: Frontal lobe
structures.function: Voluntary movement, executive function, personality, speech production (Broca's).

structures.name: Parietal lobe
structures.function: Somatosensory processing and spatial awareness.

structures.name: Temporal lobe
structures.function: Hearing, memory, and language comprehension (Wernicke's).

structures.name: Occipital lobe
structures.function: Visual processing.

structures.name: Cerebellum
structures.function: Coordination, balance, and fine motor control.

structures.name: Corpus callosum
structures.function: Large white-matter tract connecting the two cerebral hemispheres.

structures.name: Thalamus
structures.function: Relay station for sensory and motor signals to the cortex.

structures.name: Hypothalamus
structures.function: Homeostasis — controls the pituitary, temperature, hunger, thirst.

structures.name: Pituitary gland
structures.function: Master endocrine gland, hangs from the hypothalamus.

structures.name: Midbrain
structures.function: Uppermost brainstem; visual/auditory reflexes.

structures.name: Pons
structures.function: Bridges cerebrum and cerebellum; relays and helps control breathing.

structures.name: Medulla oblongata
structures.function: Controls heart rate, breathing, and blood pressure.

structures.name: Optic chiasm
structures.function: Crossing point of the optic nerves (CN II).

structures.name: Lateral ventricle
structures.function: CSF-filled cavity within each cerebral hemisphere.

structures.name: Trachea
structures.function: Conducts air to the bronchi; held open by C-shaped hyaline cartilage rings.

structures.name: Lungs
structures.function: Site of gas exchange (right lung 3 lobes, left 2).

structures.name: Diaphragm
structures.function: Primary muscle of breathing; separates thorax from abdomen.

structures.name: Liver
structures.function: Largest gland; produces bile, processes nutrients, detoxifies.

structures.name: Gallbladder
structures.function: Stores and concentrates bile.

structures.name: Stomach
structures.function: Mixes food with gastric juice; begins protein digestion.

structures.name: Spleen
structures.function: Largest lymphatic organ; filters blood, recycles RBCs.

structures.name: Pancreas
structures.function: Endocrine (islets → insulin/glucagon) and exocrine (digestive enzymes).

structures.name: Duodenum
structures.function: First part of the small intestine; receives bile and pancreatic juice.

structures.name: Small intestine
structures.function: Main site of digestion and nutrient absorption (villi).

structures.name: Large intestine
structures.function: Absorbs water and electrolytes; forms feces.

structures.name: Kidneys
structures.function: Filter blood, form urine (nephrons); regulate fluid/electrolytes.

structures.name: Adrenal glands
structures.function: Sit atop kidneys; cortex → steroids, medulla → epinephrine.

structures.name: Ureters
structures.function: Carry urine from kidneys to the bladder.

structures.name: Urinary bladder
structures.function: Stores urine; lined by transitional epithelium.

structures.name: Abdominal aorta
structures.function: Main artery of the abdomen; branches to the viscera and lower limbs.
